title: Formal Logic & Deduction
description: Master syllogisms, truth matrices, and valid conditional proofs.

puzzles.title: Deductive Syllogisms
puzzles.question: If all A are B, and some B are C... Are all A definitely C?

options.text: It is impossible to tell precisely without data mapping.

puzzles.explanation: Think of Venn Diagrams. All of Circle A sits inside Circle B. Circle C overlaps with *some* parts of Circle B, but it doesn't necessarily have to touch Circle A at all!

puzzles.title: The Modus Ponens Conditional
puzzles.question: Given statement: 'If it rains, the grass is wet.' You observe the grass is wet. Can you conclude it rained?

options.text: Yes, logical dependency is fully validated.
No, this is the fallacy of affirming the consequent.
Yes, unless the system parameters explicitly state otherwise.

puzzles.explanation: Affirming the consequent is a formal fallacy. The grass could be wet because of a sprinkler, a broken pipe, or dew—not necessarily rain!

title: Visual Mathematics
description: Understand calculus and linear transformations through clean geometric animations.

puzzles.title: Linear Vector Spaces
puzzles.question: What happens to a 2D vector matrix transformed by an identity matrix scaling property?

options.text: It collapses into a singular 1D vector line subspace.

puzzles.explanation: An identity matrix acts like the number 1 in basic multiplication; multiplying any vector map by it outputs the exact same coordinate parameters.

puzzles.title: The Essence of Derivative Rates
puzzles.question: What mathematical structure does the derivative of a function represent graph-wise at a single exact point?

options.text: The total bounded area accumulating below the curve line.
The slope of the tangent line touching that specific point coordinates.
The absolute distance separating local coordinate maximum limits.

puzzles.explanation: The derivative yields the instantaneous rate of change, geometrically represented as the slope of the line tangent to the curve at that exact location.

title: Classical Mechanics Intuition
description: Build physical mental models of inertia, momentum, and kinetic tracking layouts.

puzzles.title: Inertial Reference Coordinates
puzzles.question: If you drop a ball inside a steadily moving train car moving at constant speed, where does it land?

options.text: Directly below your hand, relative to the train floor.
Significantly behind you, due to horizontal drag parameters.
Slightly ahead of you, due to forward momentum acceleration spikes.

puzzles.explanation: Due to inertia, the ball shares the exact same forward velocity component as you and the train car. To you, it drops perfectly straight down.

puzzles.title: Conservation of Momentum Parameters
puzzles.question: An astronaut floats at rest in space and throws a heavy wrench away. What happens to the astronaut?

options.text: They remain motionless because space has no gravity tracking strings.
They immediately begin moving in the opposite direction of the wrench throw.
They spin wildly in a fixed circle directly where the action occurred.

puzzles.explanation: Conservation of total momentum dictates that if the wrench gains forward momentum vectors, the astronaut must inherit an equal and opposite backward momentum tracking rate.
